rulings.Case Title: JUDITH SHRUM et al VS. 3M COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Defendant Mack Trucks, Inc. To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To San Francisco Standard Asbestos Case Interrogatories (Loss Of Consortium), (Wrongful Death), And (Friction); Standard Document Production Request; And Request For Sanction
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, May 22, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Mack Trucks, Inc.¿s motion to compel plaintiffs¿ responses to San Francisco Standard Asbestos Case Interrogatories (loss of consortium, wrongful death, and friction) and standard document production request is denied as moot. Plaintiffs have served responses to the requested discovery. Defendant¿s request for monetary sanctions is granted in the amount of $2120.00. Plaintiffs failed to serve timely responses to the discovery dictated by this court¿s Case Management Order and only served such responses after defendant filed this motion. (See CRC 3.1348.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m., not 9:00a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
